fever.description: May indicate a viral or bacterial infection.
fever.advice: Stay hydrated, rest, and monitor temperature.
fever.when_to_seek_help: If fever lasts more than 3 days or exceeds 102°F.
follow_up: How long have you had the fever?
Do you also have chills or sweating?

headache.description: Can be caused by stress, dehydration, or lack of sleep.
headache.advice: Rest in a quiet place and drink fluids.
follow_up: Is the pain sharp or dull?
Do you have sensitivity to light?

cough.description: May result from infection or irritation.
cough.advice: Stay hydrated and avoid cold air.
follow_up: Is it dry or with mucus?
How long have you had the cough?

cold.description: Common viral infection affecting the nose and throat.

stomach pain.description: Could be due to indigestion, gas, or infection.
stomach pain.advice: Eat light food and stay hydrated.
follow_up: Where exactly is the pain located?

fatigue.description: Often caused by lack of sleep or stress.

sore throat.advice: Drink warm fluids and avoid irritants.

nausea.description: May be due to infection or food-related issues.
nausea.advice: Eat light meals and stay hydrated.

vomiting.description: Can result from infections or food poisoning.

diarrhea.description: Often caused by infection or contaminated food.
diarrhea.advice: Stay hydrated and avoid spicy food.

chest pain.description: Could indicate serious heart or lung conditions.

shortness of breath.advice: Seek immediate medical help if severe.

dizziness.description: Can result from dehydration or low blood pressure.

back pain.description: Often due to strain or poor posture.

joint pain.description: May be due to inflammation or injury.

skin rash.description: Could be due to allergy or infection.
skin rash.advice: Keep area clean and avoid scratching.

constipation.description: Often caused by low fiber diet.

heartburn.advice: Avoid spicy foods and eat smaller meals.
